questions.q: What is a charkha?
questions.explanation: The charkha is a hand-operated spinning wheel used to make yarn.

questions.q: Which Indian leader made the charkha a central symbol of the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: Gandhi made the charkha a symbol of self-reliance and the national movement.

questions.q: The charkha is used to produce what?
questions.explanation: The charkha spins fibre such as cotton into yarn or thread.

questions.q: The yarn spun on the charkha was woven into which freedom-era cloth?
questions.explanation: Charkha-spun yarn was woven into khadi, the cloth of the freedom movement.

questions.q: The charkha symbolized Gandhi's vision of what?
questions.explanation: The charkha stood for self-reliance and the empowerment of rural India.

questions.q: The charkha became one of the most recognized symbols of which struggle?
questions.explanation: The charkha became an enduring emblem of India's freedom movement.

questions.q: The charkha appeared on which national emblem during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: The charkha was featured on early versions of the Indian national flag.

questions.q: Spinning on the charkha was meant to give extra income to whom?
questions.explanation: The charkha was meant to provide supplementary income to India's poor villagers.

questions.q: The charkha worked as an alternative to which kind of cloth production?
questions.explanation: The charkha offered an Indian, self-reliant alternative to foreign machine-made textiles.

questions.q: Gandhi encouraged people to spend part of each day doing what with a charkha?
questions.explanation: Gandhi urged people to spend time daily spinning yarn on the charkha.

questions.q: The charkha occupied the centre of which pre-independence national flag?
questions.explanation: The Congress 'Swaraj' flag of the 1920s-30s carried a charkha at its centre.

questions.q: Who designed the early flag featuring the charkha that was presented to Gandhi in 1921?
questions.explanation: Pingali Venkayya designed the flag with the charkha that Gandhi endorsed in 1921.

questions.q: On the national flag of independent India, the charkha was replaced by what symbol?
questions.explanation: In 1947 the charkha was replaced by the Ashoka Chakra on the national flag.

questions.q: Gandhi popularized a small, portable spinning wheel that could fold into a box, known as the what?
questions.explanation: Gandhi popularized the portable box charkha (peti charkha) for easy spinning anywhere.

questions.q: During which movement did mass spinning on the charkha become a major nationalist activity?
questions.explanation: Charkha spinning spread widely during the Non-Cooperation Movement of the early 1920s.

questions.q: The charkha was a core part of Gandhi's wider programme called what?
questions.explanation: Spinning on the charkha was central to Gandhi's Constructive Programme.

questions.q: Which 1925 organization was set up partly to spread charkha spinning and khadi?
questions.explanation: The All India Spinners' Association, founded in 1925, promoted spinning and khadi.

questions.q: Gandhi saw daily charkha spinning as a way to dignify what?
questions.explanation: Gandhi believed spinning dignified manual labour and bound classes together.

questions.q: The charkha symbolized resistance to economic dependence on which country's textile mills?
questions.explanation: The charkha symbolized resistance to dependence on British textile mills.

questions.q: Why did Gandhi favour the charkha as a tool for the poorest Indians?
options: It was cheap, simple, and usable at home
questions.explanation: The charkha was cheap, simple, and could be used in any home, suiting the rural poor.

questions.q: The charkha embodied Gandhi's preference for what scale of production?
questions.explanation: The charkha reflected Gandhi's belief in decentralized, village-based cottage production.

questions.q: Many Congress members were at times required to send in a quota of yarn spun on the charkha as a sign of what?
questions.explanation: Spinning yarn quotas became, at times, a token of commitment for Congress workers.

questions.q: Gandhi offered a cash prize in the 1920s for an improved charkha design that could boost spinning output. What was the broad aim?
options: To replace it with a power loom
questions.explanation: Gandhi sought an improved charkha to raise hand-spinning efficiency while keeping it manual.

questions.q: Which multi-spindle improved spinning wheel, developed in the 1950s and named for the sky, was promoted to raise a spinner's output?
questions.explanation: The Ambar charkha, a multi-spindle improved spinning wheel introduced in the 1950s, was promoted to raise hand-spinning output.

questions.q: The charkha on the 1931 Karachi-era Congress tricolour was placed on which colour band?
questions.explanation: On the 1931 Congress tricolour the charkha was placed on the central white band.

questions.q: Gandhi argued that the charkha could counter which colonial economic process he and others described?
options: The drain of wealth and destruction of indigenous industry
questions.explanation: Gandhi saw the charkha as a counter to the drain of wealth and the ruin of indigenous industry.

questions.q: Which raw material is most commonly fed into a charkha to be spun into thread?
questions.explanation: Carded cotton, formed into a sliver or pooni, is most commonly spun on the charkha.

questions.q: Gandhi's daily spinning practice was sometimes described by him as a form of what beyond economics?
questions.explanation: Gandhi regarded spinning as a meditative, almost spiritual discipline, not merely an economic act.

questions.q: In debates with which leader did Gandhi defend the charkha against the case for large-scale industrialization?
questions.explanation: Gandhi and Nehru differed over the charkha-centred village economy versus heavy industrialization.

questions.q: The charkha's prominence on the national flag was finally changed at which 1947 body's decision?
questions.explanation: In 1947 the Constituent Assembly adopted the flag with the Ashoka Chakra in place of the charkha.
